conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I used to think that regular expression matching and keyword search engines like Elasticsearch were fully sufficient for building a production-grade documentation search.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Keyword search handles exact matches incredibly fast and reliably.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: But it misses all semantic nuances. Once vector embeddings became mainstream, I completely discarded keyword search. I became convinced that pure dense vector retrieval using cosine similarity on high-dimensional embeddings was the only modern solution.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Vector search is great for capturing user intent and synonyms.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: However, pure vector search fails miserably on exact part numbers and serial codes, which our users search for constantly. I've evolved my viewpoint. I now firmly believe that a hybrid search engine combining BM25 keyword matching with dense vectors via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) is the absolute best approach.

query: What is the user's current stance on the best architecture for building a search system?
